category_sources: Official Azul jHiccup page describes it as an open source tool for measuring pauses and stalls in the Java runtime platform.
source_facts.description says jHiccup measures pauses and stalls of an app's Java runtime platform.
display_name_sources: source_facts.executables include jHiccup; official Azul page uses the styled name jHiccup.
docs_sources: Official Azul documentation has a dedicated Using jHiccup page.
Official Azul product/component page includes jHiccup usage and FAQ information.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo is empty; public references from the project author and official Azul materials identify the jHiccup source repository as giltene/jHiccup.
tags_sources: Official Azul documentation explains jHiccup measures JVM/runtime and operating-system pauses.
source_facts.description identifies Java runtime pause and stall measurement.
